body.before: As a developer, I want consistent code formatting and linting rules enforced across both the Dart and C# codebases so that code style is uniform and common mistakes are caught at edit time.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-1 (repo scaffolding — needs both Flutter and .NET projects to exist)\n\nConfigure linting and formatting rules for both Dart and C# immediately after scaffolding so all subsequent code follows consistent standards.

body.before: As a developer, I want a clean configuration strategy with environment-specific settings and secure secret handling so that the app works correctly in local, Docker, and production environments without hardcoded values.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-1 (repo scaffolding)\n\nSet up strongly-typed configuration for the .NET backend and environment-specific settings for Flutter before any feature work begins.

body.before: As a developer, I want a Docker Compose stack that runs PostgreSQL, Redis, and the .NET API locally so that I can develop and test without external dependencies.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-1 (repo scaffolding — .NET project must exist for Dockerfile)\n\nDocker Compose for local dev with PostgreSQL, Redis, and .NET API.

body.before: As a developer, I want a Firebase project configured with FCM enabled, service account credentials generated, and the Flutter app registered so that push notification infrastructure is ready before Phase 2 begins.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-1 (repo scaffolding — Flutter project must exist to add Firebase config files)\n\nFirebase project setup. Can be done in parallel with Docker Compose work.

body.before: As a developer, I want a repeatable database migration workflow using EF Core so that schema changes are version-controlled, auditable, and safely applied to production.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-1 (.NET project), US-GCG-2 (Docker Compose — needs PostgreSQL running locally)\n\nEF Core migrations and initial schema. Must be done before ScrapedDuck ingestion (US-GCG-4).

body.before: As a developer, I want signing certificates and provisioning profiles configured for both platforms so that I can build release-mode apps and test on physical devices from day one.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-1 (Flutter project must exist for signing config)\n\nCan be done in parallel with backend work. Must be done before app store submission (US-GCG-15).

body.before: As a developer, I want structured logging with Serilog and meaningful error tracking so that I can diagnose issues in production and monitor the ingestion pipeline health.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-1 (.NET project)\n\nSet up Serilog early so all subsequent backend work has proper logging from the start.

body.before: As the .NET API, I want a Redis cache service with cache-aside pattern so that event data is served fast from memory with automatic fallback to PostgreSQL on cache miss.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-1 (.NET project), US-GCG-2 (Docker Compose — Redis must be running)\n\nRedis cache service. Must be done before ingestion (US-GCG-4) and API endpoints (US-GCG-5).

body.before: As a developer, I want a scheduled background job that fetches Pokemon Go event data from ScrapedDuck, normalises it, and stores it in PostgreSQL with a Redis cache so that the app always has fresh event data to serve.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-18 (DB migrations — schema must exist), US-GCG-20 (data contract defined), US-GCG-21 (Redis cache service)\n\nCore ingestion pipeline. Must be done before REST API (US-GCG-5).

body.before: As a Pokemon Go player, I want all event times displayed in my local timezone so that "2pm Community Day" means 2pm where I am, matching how Pokemon Go actually works.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-20 (data contract — need to understand how ScrapedDuck represents times), US-GCG-4 (event schema — need to know how events are stored)\n\nTimezone logic for both backend and frontend. Must be done before daily calendar view (US-GCG-7).

body.before: As the Flutter app, I want REST endpoints to fetch current and upcoming events so that I can display today's active buffs and upcoming events to the user.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-4 (ingestion — events must exist in DB), US-GCG-21 (Redis cache), US-GCG-22 (timezone handling), US-GCG-29 (logging)\n\nREST API endpoints. Must be done before Flutter app can display real data (US-GCG-7).

body.before: As the system, I want the backend to handle failures in external dependencies gracefully so that the app continues serving data even when ScrapedDuck or Redis are unavailable.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-4 (ScrapedDuck client — need existing HTTP client to add Polly), US-GCG-21 (Redis cache — need cache service to add fallback logic)\n\nResilience patterns (retry, circuit breaker, graceful degradation).

body.before: As a developer, I want the REST API versioned and documented with OpenAPI/Swagger so that the API contract is explicit and the Flutter app can evolve independently from the backend.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-5 (REST API — need existing endpoints to version and document)\n\nAPI versioning and Swagger documentation.

body.before: As a developer, I want the .NET API to auto-deploy to Coolify on push to main so that production stays up to date without manual intervention.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-2 (Docker Compose — production Dockerfile based on dev), US-GCG-19 (env config — Coolify needs env vars defined)\n\nDeploy backend to Coolify. Can be done once backend is functional.

body.before: As a user, I want to open the app and see a clean, well-structured interface so that I can navigate to the daily event view quickly.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-1 (Flutter project scaffolded), US-GCG-20 (data contract — for Dart models)\n\nFlutter app structure, state management, theme, and API service layer. Must be done before any UI screens.

body.before: As a user, I want clear feedback when the app is loading, when there are no events, or when something goes wrong so that I'm never confused about what's happening.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-6 (Flutter scaffold — app structure and theme must exist)\n\nReusable loading, error, and empty state widgets. Build these before feature screens so they can be used everywhere.

body.before: As a Pokemon Go player, I want to see today's active events and buffs at a glance so that I can plan my play session in under 5 seconds.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-6 (Flutter scaffold), US-GCG-5 (REST API — need real endpoints), US-GCG-22 (timezone — need timezone conversion for display), US-GCG-27 (error states — for loading/error/empty handling)\n\nThe hero screen of the app. Must be done before flagging, upcoming, filtering, and onboarding.

body.before: As a Pokemon Go player, I want event data to be available offline so that I can check today's buffs without a network connection.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-6 (Flutter scaffold — API service layer), US-GCG-5 (REST API — need endpoints to sync from)\n\nOffline-first data layer. Must be done before data freshness indicator (US-GCG-26).

body.before: As a Pokemon Go player, I want to flag events I care about so that I can quickly identify my priority events in the calendar view.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-7 (daily calendar view — need event cards to add flag toggle to)\n\nOn-device flagging. Must be done before push notification scheduling (US-GCG-11) which syncs flags to backend.

body.before: As a Pokemon Go player, I want to see upcoming events for the next week so that I can plan my play sessions in advance and know what's coming.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-7 (daily calendar — reuses event card component and screen patterns)

body.before: As a Pokemon Go player, I want to filter events by type (community day, raid hour, etc.) so that I can quickly find the events most relevant to my playstyle.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-7 (daily calendar — need event list screens to add filters to)

body.before: As a Pokemon Go player, I want to see when event data was last updated so that I know whether I'm looking at current information or stale cached data.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-9 (offline sync — need sync tracking to show freshness)

body.before: As a new user, I want a brief onboarding flow on first launch so that I understand what the app does and how to use flagging before I start.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-7 (daily calendar — onboarding references the daily view as destination)\n\nBuild last among frontend features so the onboarding screenshots/descriptions reflect the final UI.

body.before: As a developer, I want a test project with unit and integration test patterns established so that every backend feature can be tested from day one.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-1 (.NET project), US-GCG-5 (REST API — need code to test), US-GCG-4 (ingestion — need ScrapedDuck client to test)\n\nBackend test infrastructure. Must be done before CI pipeline (US-GCG-32).

body.before: As a developer, I want a Flutter testing setup with unit tests for services and widget tests for key screens so that UI and business logic regressions are caught early.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-6 (Flutter scaffold), US-GCG-7 (daily calendar — need screens to widget-test), US-GCG-22 (timezone — need timezone logic to unit-test)\n\nFlutter test infrastructure. Must be done before CI pipeline (US-GCG-32).

body.before: As a developer, I want a CI pipeline that runs linting, formatting, and tests on every push so that code quality is enforced automatically before merging or deploying.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-30 (.NET test infra), US-GCG-31 (Flutter test infra)\n\nGitHub Actions CI. Must be done before E2E testing (US-GCG-35).

body.before: As the app, I want to register the device's FCM token with the backend so that the server can send push notifications to this device.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-16 (Firebase setup — credentials needed), US-GCG-6 (Flutter scaffold — app must exist), US-GCG-5 (REST API — need backend to register tokens with)\n\nFCM token registration. Must be done before notification scheduling (US-GCG-11) and Flutter handling (US-GCG-12).

body.before: As a Pokemon Go player, I want to receive a push notification before a flagged event ends so that I don't miss my window to take advantage of active buffs.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-10 (FCM registration — need device tokens stored), US-GCG-8 (event flagging — flags drive notification scheduling), US-GCG-16 (Firebase — need Admin SDK for sending)\n\nServer-side notification scheduling with timezone awareness.

body.before: As a Pokemon Go player, I want push notifications to appear correctly on my device and open the relevant event when tapped so that I can quickly act on the notification.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-10 (FCM registration — token must be registered to receive notifications)\n\nFlutter-side notification display and deep linking.

body.before: As a Pokemon Go player, I want a settings screen where I can control notification preferences so that I only get notified about the event types I care about and at the right time before they end.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-11 (notification scheduling — need backend preferences support), US-GCG-12 (Flutter handling — need notification system working)\n\nSettings screen for notification preferences. Last notification story.

body.before: As a developer, I want end-to-end tests that verify the full flow from ScrapedDuck ingestion through the API to the Flutter app so that I have confidence the system works as a whole before releasing.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-32 (CI pipeline), US-GCG-5 (REST API), US-GCG-7 (daily calendar), US-GCG-2 (Docker Compose)\n\nEnd-to-end tests. Near the end of the sprint after all features are built.

body.before: As a developer, I want app icons, splash screens, store screenshots, and listing metadata prepared so that the app can be submitted to both stores.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-7 (daily calendar — need app built to capture screenshots)\n\nStore assets, splash screen, app icons, privacy policy.

body.before: As a developer, I want to profile the app's performance and review accessibility so that it meets store quality standards and is usable by all players.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-7 (daily calendar), US-GCG-23 (upcoming events), US-GCG-28 (notification preferences — full app must be built to profile)\n\nPerformance and accessibility audit before store submission.

body.before: As a developer, I want to submit the app to the iOS App Store and Google Play so that users can download it.
body.after: **Requires:** US-GCG-13 (store assets), US-GCG-14 (performance review), US-GCG-17 (app signing)\n\nFinal story. Submit to both app stores.
